headline: Sony’s PlayStation disc factory is already being repurposed
summary: Sony's PlayStation disc factory in Austria is being repurposed. The plant, which once produced 600,000 discs daily, is shifting focus. This move signals Sony's strategic shift away from physical game media.

headline: Tesla driver faces manslaughter charges over Texas crash that killed a woman inside her home
summary: A Tesla driver faces manslaughter charges after his car struck and killed a woman in her Texas home. Michael Butler, 44, was arrested following the fatal accident last month. He claimed to have been using Tesla's Full-Self Driving (FSD) at the time of the crash.

headline: Meta has a new app called Pocket, an AI-focused "gizmo" creator.
summary: Meta has launched a new app called Pocket, focusing on AI-driven interactive content. Users can create and share 'gizmos' built from AI prompts within the app. This new Pocket is entirely separate from Mozilla's older 'read-it-later' app of the same name.

headline: The best July 4th tech sales available now.
summary: Many tech deals are still available through July 4th, despite Prime Day having concluded. These sales often preview mid-July discounts, offering a second chance for shoppers. Retailers like Best Buy continue to feature a variety of promotions.

headline: Spider-Man: Brand New Day director reveals new villain looks.
summary: Director Destin Daniel Cretton has offered new glimpses of villains in 'Spider-Man: Brand New Day.' Fans get their best look yet at characters like Boomerang and The Hand. Speculation is rising about whether the revealed photos might be a misdirection.

headline: Brain Training professor says manual cars boost brain health.
summary: Professor Ryuta Kawashima suggests driving a manual transmission car benefits brain health. The neuroscientist, known for Nintendo's Brain Age games, indicates it could help prevent dementia. A study led by Kawashima supports this cognitive advantage over automatic cars.

headline: Suicide Squad developers considered quitting after game release.
summary: Two developers from 'Suicide Squad: Kill the Justice League' shared their negative experiences with the game's production. They revealed they considered leaving the industry after the game's release. Their transparent account highlights significant challenges faced during development.

headline: Cape Fear Episode 6: "Possum" is a hilarious, action-packed acid trip.
summary: Episode 6 of Apple TV’s 'Cape Fear,' titled 'Possum,' has been released. The review describes the episode as a hilarious, action-packed acid trip. Critics suggest it successfully puts the thriller series back on track.

headline: NHS launches "marathon a month" scheme to reward walking.
summary: The NHS is launching a new initiative to reward people for walking 30 minutes a day. Named the "marathon a month" scheme, it will begin early next year. This program was developed with former Olympic medallist Sir Brendan Foster.

headline: BBC reports uneasy calm in Strait of Hormuz, finds seized ships.
summary: BBC journalists were the first international reporters to visit Bandar Abbas on the Iranian side of the Strait of Hormuz. They observed seized ships and shark fishermen, noting the complex situation. The report describes an uneasy calm returning to the vital shipping lane.

headline: Burnham signals slight flexibility on tax, but pledges remain.
summary: Andy Burnham, likely the next UK Prime Minister, suggested there is 'some room for movement' on tax policy. However, he reaffirmed Labour's commitment to not raise VAT, income tax, or national insurance. This stance aims to balance fiscal flexibility with voter assurance.

headline: France records 2,025 excess heatwave deaths; Europe braces for more.
summary: France reported 2,025 excess deaths during the peak of its recent heatwave. Forecasters are warning of further extreme temperatures across the European continent. Europe is bracing for more severe weather conditions in the coming days.
